summary: The most operationally significant development is the direct military exchange between Iran and the United States, with US forces striking multiple Iranian military positions in Qeshm, Sirik, Bandar Lengeh, and Bandar Kong in response to an Iranian drone attack on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's Assembly of Experts issued an unprecedented statement setting hardline conditions for any negotiations, including maintaining the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and rejecting discussion of nuclear rights. Simultaneously, Israel and Lebanon reached an agreement with US backing that Israel's Prime Minister characterized as a diplomatic achievement, while a terrorist infiltration incident occurred at Beit Arie in the West Bank.
key_points: US forces struck Iranian military positions on Qeshm Island, Sirik, Bandar Lengeh, and Bandar Kong in response to an Iranian drone attack on a vessel transiting the Strait of Hormuz from Qatar to Fujairah. (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74439, @asafroz/50806, @naya_foriraq/80106)

Iran's Assembly of Experts, with 62 of 86 members signing, issued a message to government officials demanding the Strait of Hormuz remain closed, rejecting nuclear rights discussions, and calling for US withdrawal from the region as non-negotiable positions. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373195)

An Iranian drone attack targeted a ship in Omani coastal waters near the Strait of Hormuz, and Iran also launched two drones at Bahrain, with one intercepted and one falling without causing damage. (Source: @mamlekate/103766, @New_security8200/202119)

Prime Minister Netanyahu stated that Israel and Lebanon reached an agreement with US and Lebanese recognition that Israel would remain on the yellow line, calling it a major blow to Iran and Hezbollah. (Source: @almogboker78/17610, @almogboker78/17609)

A terrorist infiltration incident occurred at Beit Arie Tzofar in Samaria, beginning around 04:00 when a resident identified two masked individuals, prompting a military search operation that was declared concluded at 11:19. (Source: @New_security8200/202110, @inon_yttach/9178, @tzevaadom_en/68506)

US military activity increased around the Strait of Hormoz with E-3G Sentry AWACS from Prince Sultan Air Base, KC-135Rs from Tel Aviv, Al Udeid, and Abu Dhabi, plus MQ-4C Triton from Al-Azraq and stealth aircraft. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373221)
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is expected to visit Iraq tomorrow, signaling regional diplomatic engagement amid the military escalation. (Source: @asafroz/50796)

The family nucleus of the settlement of Noa in the northern West Bank arrived on-site for the first time, marking a new settlement beyond those evacuated under the disengagement. (Source: @amitsegal/62575)

sections.executive_summary: US forces conducted strikes on multiple Iranian military positions including Qeshm Island, Sirik, Bandar Lengeh, and Bandar Kong in retaliation for an Iranian drone attack on a commercial vessel transiting the Strait of Hormuz from Qatar to Fujairah. Iran's Assembly of Experts issued an unprecedented hardline statement demanding the Strait of Hormuz remain closed, rejecting any discussion of nuclear rights, and insisting on US withdrawal from the region as non-negotiable conditions. Israel and Lebanon reached a US-brokered agreement that Netanyahu characterized as a diplomatic achievement against Iran and Hezbollah, while a separate terrorist infiltration incident occurred at Beit Arie in the West Bank.

points: Iran launched a drone attack on a commercial ship transiting from Qatar to Fujairah through the Strait of Hormuz, and also launched two drones at Bahrain, with one intercepted from the ground and one falling without damage. (Source: @mamlekate/103766, @New_security8200/202119)

Iran's Assembly of Experts, with 62 of 88 members signing, issued an unprecedented message to government officials setting non-negotiable conditions including keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed, rejecting discussion of nuclear rights, demanding war reparations, release of frozen assets, lifting of all sanctions, and US withdrawal from the region. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373195)

Explosions were reported in southern Iran near the Strait of Hormuz, specifically in Sirik in Hormozgan Province, as well as on Qeshm Island, Bandar Lengeh, and Bandar Kong, following US strikes on Iranian military positions. (Source: @shin_persian/7237, @Faytuks_Network/4435, @asafroz/50806, @naya_foriraq/80106)
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is expected to visit Iraq tomorrow, indicating active Iranian diplomatic engagement in the region amid military escalation. (Source: @asafroz/50796)

Bloomberg reported, citing informed sources, that a US attack on a school in Minab, southern Iran on the first day of the war against Tehran was caused by a deficiency in the Pentagon's target analysis system, with an analyst's correction that the site was a school rather than an IRGC naval facility being ignored by higher authorities. (Source: @SharghDaily/168461)

Prime Minister Netanyahu stated that Israel and Lebanon reached an agreement, with both the US and Lebanon telling Iran 'this is not your affair,' and characterized the Lebanese government's signing as a major blow to Iran and Hezbollah. (Source: @almogboker78/17610, @almogboker78/17609)

Netanyahu emphasized that the test of the agreement would be on the ground, dependent on freedom of IDF operational capacity, including removing emerging threats by attacking and destroying Hezbollah's attempts to rearm, not only when they attack Israeli forces or settlements. (Source: @almogboker78/17609)

A terrorist infiltration incident occurred at Beit Arie Tzofar in Samaria around 04:00, when a resident identified two masked individuals outside his home who fled upon being called; a military night operation was declared and large forces from the brigade and border police conducted searches. (Source: @inon_yttach/9178, @New_security8200/202110)

The family nucleus of the settlement of Noa in the northern West Bank arrived on-site for the first time, representing a new settlement beyond those previously evacuated under the disengagement plan. (Source: @amitsegal/62575)
Trump spoke by phone with the Lebanese President and congratulated him on signing the agreement with Israel, confirming US diplomatic engagement in the Israel-Lebanon track. (Source: @N12chat/214870)

A terrorist infiltration incident at Beit Arie Tzofar in Samaria began around 04:00 when a resident identified two masked individuals outside his home; the individuals fled when called, prompting a military cordon and extensive search by brigade and border police forces. (Source: @inon_yttach/9178, @tzevaadom_en/68506)

The Beit Arie infiltration incident was declared concluded at 11:19 on 27/06/2026, with the military operation ending after searches of the settlement area and house yards. (Source: @New_security8200/202110)

The family nucleus of the settlement of Noa in the northern West Bank arrived on-site for the first time, marking the establishment of a new settlement location beyond those previously evacuated under the disengagement. (Source: @amitsegal/62575)

Lebanon signed an agreement with Israel, with President Trump congratulating the Lebanese President by phone on the signing; the agreement includes recognition by both the US and Lebanon that Israel will remain on the yellow line. (Source: @N12chat/214870, @almogboker78/17610)

Netanyahu stated that Hezbollah will not disarm, the Lebanese government lacks the power to dismantle it, and therefore Israel must maintain operational freedom to remove threats, including destroying Hezbollah's attempts to rearm and re-equip. (Source: @almogboker78/17609)

The Iran Assembly of Experts' statement referenced continued Israeli ceasefire violations in Lebanon and failure to withdraw from occupied territories as justification for keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373195)
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is expected to visit Iraq tomorrow, indicating active Iranian diplomatic engagement with Iraq amid the broader regional escalation. (Source: @asafroz/50796)
A security source stated that the closure of the Green Zone in Baghdad's capital is for a security exercise and is not linked to any emergency or exceptional event. (Source: @naya_foriraq/80110)

Iran launched two drones at Bahrain, with one intercepted from the ground and one falling without causing damage, extending the conflict's direct military dimension to Gulf states beyond the US-Iran exchange. (Source: @New_security8200/202119, @shin_persian/7232)

An Iranian drone attacked a commercial vessel transiting from Qatar to Fujairah through the Strait of Hormuz, bringing direct military confrontation into Gulf state waters and the critical Hormuz chokepoint. (Source: @mamlekate/103766, @New_security8200/202119)

US military activity around the Strait of Hormuz increased with E-3G Sentry AWACS from Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, KC-135Rs from Tel Aviv, Al Udeid in Qatar, and Abu Dhabi, plus MQ-4C Triton from Al-Azraq and stealth aircraft. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373221)

A US C-130H Hercules transport aircraft with call sign BNDGE57, typically used for medical evacuation and casualty transport, was observed departing Yanbu airport in Saudi Arabia heading east. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373229)

key_developments: US forces struck Iranian military positions on Qeshm Island, Sirik, Bandar Lengeh, and Bandar Kong in retaliation for an Iranian drone attack on a vessel transiting the Strait of Hormuz from Qatar to Fujairah. (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74439, @asafroz/50806, @naya_foriraq/80106)

Iran's Assembly of Experts, with 62 of 88 members signing, issued an unprecedented statement demanding the Strait of Hormuz remain closed, rejecting nuclear rights discussions, and insisting on US withdrawal from the region. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373195)
Iran launched two drones at Bahrain, with one intercepted and one falling without damage, extending direct military action to Gulf states. (Source: @New_security8200/202119)

Israel and Lebanon reached a US-brokered agreement with Israel remaining on the yellow line, which Netanyahu called a major blow to Iran and Hezbollah. (Source: @almogboker78/17610, @almogboker78/17609)

A terrorist infiltration incident at Beit Arie Tzofar in Samaria was concluded after a military operation, with two masked individuals identified by a resident around 04:00. (Source: @New_security8200/202110, @inon_yttach/9178)

US military activity around the Strait of Hormuz increased with AWACS from Saudi Arabia, tankers from multiple bases including Qatar and the UAE, and stealth aircraft, indicating sustained operational tempo. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/373221)
Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is expected to visit Iraq tomorrow, signaling Iranian diplomatic outreach amid military escalation. (Source: @asafroz/50796)
sections.threat_assessment: The direct US-Iran military exchange marks a significant escalation, with US strikes on Qeshm, Sirik, Bandar Lengeh, and Bandar Kong representing overt attacks on Iranian sovereign territory in response to the Hormuz drone incident. Iran's Assembly of Experts statement signals regime hardliners are positioning to constrain any diplomatic off-ramp, with the demand to keep the Strait of Hormuz closed directly threatening global energy shipping. The extension of Iranian drone operations to Bahrain demonstrates willingness to bring the conflict to Gulf state territories, while increased US airborne assets around Hormuz suggest preparation for sustained operations. The Israel-Lebanon agreement creates a diplomatic front against Iran but does not reduce the immediate military escalation trajectory between Washington and Tehran.
sections.regional_response: The Israel-Lebanon agreement with US backing represents a diplomatic realignment, with Trump personally congratulating the Lebanese President and Netanyahu framing it as a blow to Iran and Hezbollah. Iraq is emerging as a diplomatic venue with Araghchi's planned visit, suggesting Baghdad may be positioning as an intermediary. The Green Zone closure in Baghdad being characterized as a routine security exercise indicates Iraq is managing domestic perception while engaging diplomatically. Bahrain's experience of Iranian drone incursion, even without damage, is likely to accelerate Gulf state security coordination with the US and Israel. The Assembly of Experts' intervention signals Iran's domestic political apparatus is actively shaping the negotiation parameters, potentially constraining the Iranian government's room for diplomatic maneuver.
sections.intelligence_notes: The Bloomberg report on the Minab school bombing attribution to a Pentagon target analysis system deficiency raises questions about US targeting processes and whether similar errors may affect ongoing operations. The C-130H Hercules with call sign BNDGE57 departing Yanbu heading east, with BNDGE typically associated with medical evacuation missions, may indicate casualty movement from the strikes. The Iranian drone attack on a vessel from Qatar to Fujairah, combined with drones at Bahrain, suggests Iran is testing responses across the Gulf, not just at US targets. The timing of US strikes at 01:30 local time indicates operational planning for minimal visibility. The Assembly of Experts' 62-of-88 member signature count suggests significant but not unanimous clerical consensus, which could shift depending on battlefield outcomes.
